message:
test(fcp-core): pin IntentStatus crash-recovery state machine (flywheel_connectors-epymi)

Bead asks for SymbolStoreState transitions per documented state
machine. fcp-core has no type literally named SymbolStoreState; the
closest documented state machine for a stored resource is
IntentStatus (operation.rs:356), used during crash recovery to
determine the state of an operation. Its 5 variants form a
documented lifecycle:

  Pending → InProgress → {Completed, Failed}   (terminal)
                       └→ Orphaned             (timeout, terminal)

IntentStatus carries #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")] and a
hand-written Display impl returning the same snake_case tokens.
Existing operation_golden_vectors.rs uses IntentStatus in fixtures
but doesn't pin its tokens, transitions, or serde shape.

Coverage:
- 5-variant count + label sentinel against silent additions
- Display token per variant pinned (pending/in_progress/completed/
  failed/orphaned)
- Display byte-for-byte agrees with serde JSON snake_case tag
- JSON + CBOR round-trip preserves variant identity
- CBOR encodes as Text (not integer) for cross-language consumers
- PascalCase + unknown + wrong-vocabulary tokens rejected
- Pairwise distinct variants and Display tokens
- Terminal vs non-terminal classification: exactly 2 non-terminal
  (Pending + InProgress) and exactly 3 terminal (Completed + Failed
  + Orphaned), with each variant individually pinned — drift in
  lifecycle semantics surfaces here
- Multi-word variant uses underscore not hyphen (in_progress)
- Every token is snake_case lowercase ASCII with no leading/
  trailing/double underscore
- Copy + Eq correctness across copies (IntentStatus does NOT derive
  Hash; distinctness via serde tag)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
